sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 5-year-old's aunt who hurt her left knee about a week ago while playing with her niece, who is learning to ride a bike.
The patient twisted her knee in a "funky way" and heard a pop.
She has been experiencing pain in the medial aspect of the knee, which she rates as a 6 out of 10 in terms of severity.
The pain has been persistent and has not improved with Tylenol, which she has been taking as needed.
The patient has a history of an ulcer and is unable to take ibuprofen.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: The patient is currently taking Tylenol as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has edema and effusion in the left knee, with moderate range of motion.
The patient experiences pain on flexion and extension of the knee.
The Lachman's test and valgus and varus tests are negative.
Test Results: The patient had a normal x-ray of the left knee with no fractures or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a suspected medial collateral ligament (MCL) strain in the left knee.
Problem list: MCL strain; status: suspected.
Plan:
I prescribed tramadol 50mg twice a day for pain management, and advised the patient to take Tylenol as needed for breakthrough pain.
I also put the patient in a knee immobilizer to be worn for the next week to reduce inflammation and allow the knee to heal.
The patient is advised to ice the knee 3-4 times a day for 20-30 minutes at a time.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up in 2 days to re-evaluate the knee and assess for improvement.
